description:
## Background and intent

This is the first user-visible frontend slice for the Issue Explorer. The product direction is a dense desktop client inspired by Linear/Raycast with an Obsidian-and-Glow visual system. The first screen should immediately feel like Beadsmith, even before real Beadwork data is wired in.

This bead exists so layout, visual hierarchy, accessibility, and design-system choices can be reviewed independently from backend and async data concerns. Keeping it static also lets the Rust adapter and reference-source work proceed in parallel.

## What to build

Replace the starter Tauri/Vite screen with the static issue explorer shell shown in `docs/research/design/mockups/issue_explorer_functional_sidebar.png`: left navigation, middle issue-list pane, and right empty-detail pane. Use `docs/research/PRD.md` and `docs/research/DESIGN.md` for the desktop-only layout direction and design tokens.

This task is layout-only. It should not call Rust, TauRPC, Effect services, raw Tauri `invoke`, or `bw`. Use local placeholder state, disabled controls, or empty-state content only where needed to make the shell visually complete. The shell should make clear which controls are inert so the UI does not imply implemented filtering or selection.

Keep the first viewport useful and dense: no marketing page, no decorative card-heavy landing screen, no hero. This is a tool surface. Use semantic HTML, accessible labels for inputs/buttons, keyboard-reachable controls where controls exist, and stable pane dimensions so the app does not shift as later data arrives.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The starter screen is replaced by a three-pane issue explorer shell matching the mockup direction.
- [ ] The shell has a left navigation pane, middle issue-list pane, and right empty-detail pane with desktop-appropriate fixed/flexible widths.
- [ ] The right pane remains in a `No issue selected` empty state.
- [ ] Search and sidebar filters are visible but inert or disabled; they do not imply implemented filtering.
- [ ] No backend command, TauRPC client, Effect service, raw Tauri `invoke`, or `bw` call is introduced in this task.
- [ ] Lucide icons are used for shell iconography if icons are introduced.
- [ ] shadcn is only added through the shadcn CLI if reusable base components are introduced in this slice; otherwise defer it.
- [ ] Text does not overlap, truncate awkwardly, or resize containers at normal desktop viewport sizes.
- [ ] The shell uses semantic and accessible controls for navigation, search, and actions.
- [ ] Relevant frontend formatting/check/build commands pass.
- [ ] A screenshot or local visual verification note is captured in the closing comment so future work knows what was inspected.

## Out of scope

- Real Beadwork data loading.
- Displaying returned `workspacePath`.
- Rendering real `IssueSummary` rows.
- Issue selection or detail behavior.
- Workspace switching or command palette behavior.

## Triage

ready-for-agent

comments.text: Planning audit: keep this bead static and visual. It should make Beadsmith feel like the intended dense desktop tool before data is wired, but must not fake implemented behavior. Disabled/inert controls are acceptable when they preserve layout intent without implying filters, selection, or workspace switching work.
Visually verified the layout with Vite dev server. The three-pane shell is static, styling matches PRD tokens exactly, right pane shows the empty state.

title: Build the static three-pane issue explorer shell
